name: Tabele Barbatilor — Census of Jewish Men in Romania, 1942
sources: Archive and Library of the Federation of Jewish Communities of Romania, in Bucharest, Fond #: III 400 E/1942.
description: Tabele Barbatilor Census of more than 20,000 Jewish men, 1942.

name: U.S. Consular Post, Bucharest, Romania — Emergency Passport Applications and other records
sources: U.S. National Archives, Record Group 84 (RG 84), State Department Foreign Service Posts
description: Emergency Passport Applications and other items - nearly 1,000 records from the U.S. State Department, 1860-1941.

name: Romanian Jews Killed and Missing in Action, World War I
sources: Evreii Din Romania in Razboiul de reintregire a tarii 1916-1919" [Jews in Romania during the war of the integration of the country 1916-1919], Dumitru Hincu and Lya Benjamin, eds., Bucuresti, Editura Hasefer, 1996
description: List of names of Romanian Jews killed in action in the First World War.

name: Memorial for the Jews of Craiova who fell in the Balkans Wars and WWI
sources: Jeff Malka Sephardic Collection, Central Archives for the History of the Jewish People in Jerusalem
description: List of names of Jews from Caiova who were killed in action in the Balkans Wars and WWI, as seen on the memorial outside of the town's only remaining synagogue.

name: The Sephardic Jews in Romania's Economic Life
sources: Jeff Malka Sephardic Collection, Hayehudim hasefaradim b'chyei hacalcala shel Romania (Peamim 1987)
description: List of list the surnames of The Sephardi Jews in Romania's economic life, taken from a 1987 article written by Yaakov Geller

name: Romanian Birth Records
sources: Romanian National Archives
description: More than 115,000 Jewish birth records from across Romania, including Iasi and Bucharest, and many other towns.

name: Romanian Marriage and Divorce Records
sources: Romanian National Archives
description: More than 27,000 Jewish marriage and divorce records from across Romania, including Iasi and Bucharest, and many other towns.

name: Romanian Death Records
sources: Romanian National Archives
description: More than 32,000 Jewish death records from across Romania, including Iasi and Bucharest, and many other towns.

name: Bukovina Birth Records
sources: Suceava County branch of the National Archives of Romania (Arhivele Naţionale ale României)

name: Bukovina Marriage Records
sources: Suceava County branch of the National Archives of Romania (Arhivele Naţionale ale României)
description: More than 6,000 Jewish marriage records from across the former Austro-Hungarian province of Bukovina

name: Bukovina Death Records
sources: Suceava County branch of the National Archives of Romania (Arhivele Naţionale ale României)
description: More than 16,000 Jewish death records from across the former Austro-Hungarian province of Bukovina

name: Máramaros Birth Records
region: Romania, Maramaros, Ukraine, Hungary, Austria, Subcarpathia
sources: Maramureș County branch of the National Archives of Romania (Arhivele Naţionale ale României)
description: More than 59,000 Jewish birth records from across historical Máramaros county, including parts of Subcarpathia.

name: Máramaros Marriage Records
region: Romania, Maramaros, Ukraine, Hungary, Austria, Subcarpathia
sources: Maramureș County branch of the National Archives of Romania (Arhivele Naţionale ale României)
description: More than 3,000 Jewish marriage records from across historical Máramaros county, including parts of Subcarpathia.

name: Máramaros Death Records
region: Romania, Maramaros, Ukraine, Hungary, Austria, Subcarpathia
sources: Maramureș County branch of the National Archives of Romania (Arhivele Naţionale ale României)
description: More than 20,000 Jewish death records from across historical Máramaros county, including parts of Subcarpathia.

name: Podu Iloaiei 1898 Census
sources: “Obştea evreiască din - Podu Iloaiei: file din istoria unui "ştetl" moldovenesc” [The Jewish community in - Podu Iloaiei: Pages from the history of a Moldavian Shtetl] (Bucureşti: Hasefer, 1990)
description: 723 residents listed in the 1898 census of Podul-Iloaei.

name: Survivors Returning to Sighet and Maramures
sources: Jewish Community Center in Sighet, Maramures, Romania
description: Names of 7,281 Shoah survivors who returned to Sighet and Maramureş.

name: Jewish Names in U.S. State Department Files (RG59) 1910-1929
sources: Central Decimal Files of the U.S. Department of State, Record Group 59 (RG 59)
description: More than 2,000 entries for Romania and Bessarabia from the Central Decimal Files of the U.S. Department of State, Record Group 59.

name: JewishGen Online Worldwide Burial Registry
region: Romania, Bessarabia, Moldavia, Transylvania, Dobrogea, Crisana, Bucovina, Banat,
sources: Varies
description: More than 280,000 burial records in Romania and Moldova, as well as in Romanian landsmanshaft cemeteries.

name: Yizkor Book Necrologies
sources: Yizkor Books
description: More than 30,000 entries from lists of Holocaust martyrs in Yizkor Books for towns in Romania and Moldova.

name: Yizkor Book Master Name Index
sources: Yizkor Books
description: More than 8,000 names indexed from Yizkor Books for towns in Romania and Moldova.

name: Bessarabia Duma Voters List Database
sources: Bessarabskie Guberniskie Vedomosti
description: More than 128,000 voters in Bessarabia, who were eligible to vote in the Russian Duma elections in 1906 and 1907.

name: Bessarabia Revision Lists
sources: Moldova State Archives in Chișinău (Kishinev) (MoldSA)
description: Records from Reviskaya Skazka – 19th and 20th centuries Czarist tax censuses – for more than 100 towns, shtetles and colonies, including: Akkerman (Cetatea Albă), Alexandreny (Alexăndreni), Beltsy (Bălţi), Bendery (Tighina), Brichany (Briceni), Khotyn (Hotin), Kishinev (Chişinău), Lipkany (Lipcani), Orgeev (Orhei), Soroki (Soroca), Teleneshty (Teleneşti), and many villages and agricultural colonies.

name: Bessarabia Birth Records
sources: National Archives of the Republic of Moldova (NARM)
description: More than 118,000 Jewish birth records from across the former Russian region of Bessarabia– primarily for Kishinev (now Chişinău, Moldova), but also for Beltsy (Bălţi), Bendery (Bender), Soroki (Soroca), Khotin, Novoselitsa (Novoselytsia), and other places.

name: Bessarabia Marriage and Divorce Records
sources: National Archives of the Republic of Moldova (NARM)
description: More than 38,000 Jewish marriage and divorce records from across the former Russian region of Bessarabia– primarily for Kishinev (now Chişinău, Moldova), but also for Beltsy (Bălţi), Bendery (Bender), Soroki (Soroca), Khotin, Novoselitsa (Novoselytsia), and other places.

name: Bessarabia Death Records
sources: National Archives of the Republic of Moldova (NARM)
description: More than 52,000 Jewish death records from across the former Russian region of Bessarabia– primarily for Kishinev (now Chişinău, Moldova), but also for Beltsy (Bălţi), Bendery (Bender), Soroki (Soroca), Khotin, Novoselitsa (Novoselytsia), and other places.

name: Bessarabia Business Directory, 1924
sources: Anuarul "Socec" al României-mari ["Socec" Annuary of the Great-Roumania]
description: More than 13,000 entries for Jewish businesses, in 705 localities in Bessarabia, from a 1924 Romanian business directory.

name: Chişinău Commercial Directory, 1940
sources: Anuaral Chişinăului pe anul 1940
description: Nearly 1,300 apparently Jewish names among government officials, professionals and owners, listed in a 1940 Chişinău commercial yearbook.

name: Russian-Jewish Fallen Soldiers of WWI
sources: Russian State Library
description: Data about 1,559 Jewish soldiers in the Russian army from Bessarabia, who were killed or wounded in the First World War.

name: Jews in Public Life in Bessarabia
sources: Bessarabia Reference Calendar”, “Akkerman Calendar” and “Kherson Calendar”
description: Records of Jews listed in the Czarist government's annual "Bessarabia Reference Calendar", "Akkerman Calendar" and "Kherson Calendar", 1862-1914.

name: Vsia Rossiia 1895 Database
sources: Vsia Rossiia, Library of Congress
description: 1,500 Jewish businesses in Bessarabia, from this 1895 Russian business directory.

name: Jewish Religious Personnel in the Russian Empire, 1853-1854
sources: Sinagogi, Molitvenne Doma i Sostoyashchie pri nikh Dolzhnostne Litsa v Cherte Evreiskoi Osedlosti i Guberniyakh Kurlyandskoi i Liflyandskoi Rossiiskoi Imperii 1853-1854
description: 281 Jewish religious personnel in Bessarabia Gubernia.

name: Tiraspol Uezd Revision Lists (1796-1858)
sources: Kherson State Archive
description: More than 8,000 records from Reviska Skazka, 1796-1858 — including Tiraspol, Dubăsari and Grigoriopol.

name: Mass Deportation from Moldova, 13-16 June 1941
sources: Deportarile in Masa Din R.S.S. Moldoveneasca Din 13-16 Iunie 1941
description: Information about 2,517 individuals deported from Moldova by the Soviet authorities in June 1941.

name: Moldavian Jewish Victims of Soviet Oppression
sources: Book of Memory: Moldovan Victims of Soviet Oppression 1941-1951
description: Phase 2, List of Jews who were victims of Soviet oppression. List of persons convicted by judicial system or deported from Moldavian SSR.

name: Surviving Jews in Bessarabia
sources: First List of Surviving Jews in Bessarabia
description: List of 1,782 Jews from Bessarabia who survived the Holocaust and returned to a Bessarabian village.

name: Vsya Rossiya Business Directory for Bessarabia
sources: Vsya Rossiya
description: Over 4,000 entries of apparently Jewish names from editions of the Russian business directory, Vsya Rossiya, for Bessarabia and Transnistria for the period 1897 through 1912.

name: 1916 Kishinev Business Directory
sources: “Life of Bessarabia”
description: More than 600 entries of apparently Jewish names listed in the 1916 Address and Reference yearbook (White Pages) "All Kishinev.

name: 1901 Klyachkin All-Russia Business Directory
sources: Klyachkin handbook
description: 544 records of Jews listed in the "1901 Klyachkin All-Russia Business Directory.

name: Bessarabia Court Records
sources: National Archives of the Republic of Moldova (NARM)
description: The database includes information about Jews involved in Court Cases in various jurisdictions, such as Kishinev, Beltsy, and other Districts, Appellate Courts and notary offices of Bessarabia of the 19th and early 20th centuries, based on the inventories of the State Archive of Republic of Moldova.

name: Bessarabia Out-Of-Town Jews
sources: National Archives of the Republic of Moldova (NARM)
description: The database contains records for Jews who were registered in Bessarabia, but lived in other gubernias: Podolia, Kherson, etc.
